text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy village of Whoville, there sat a wise old owl named Olga. Olga was known for her love of storytelling. One crisp Halloween night, she sat on her favorite branch, her feathers puffs out, telling a tale as colorful as the moonlight.

"Once," she began, gripping her storybook more tightly, "there was a small pond known far and wide as the Happy Pond. Birds of all feathered kinds gathered there, not just for the melodious songs they sang but because the water had magical properties."

As Olga glowed with pride, a squirrel rustled the leaves nearby. "Every autumn, a peculiar pumpkin would grow there, orange and glossy. It hadn't moved in years, just sat there, glowing steadily."

Olga perched thoughtfully. "You see," she mused, wiping a tear from her eye that resembled a shiny orange pumpkin seeds, "there was a little chick that wanted that pumpkin
